app.name: SolonCode
app.one_liner: An open-source Java-based coding agent supporting team/sub-agent coordination, CLI-first interaction, and multi-platform deployment (CLI, desktop, web).

screens.name: CLI Console Interface
screens.purpose: Primary user interaction point for invoking SolonCode commands and viewing agent output.
primary_actions: Run soloncode command in any workspace directory
View real-time agent reasoning and code edits

screens.name: Configuration Management
screens.purpose: Manage SolonCode settings including LLM model selection and runtime behavior.
primary_actions: Locate logs and runtime files under ~/.soloncode

rust_commands.name: configure_model
rust_commands.purpose: Update the chatModel configuration in config.yml to switch LLM providers or models.

rust_commands.name: run_soloncode
rust_commands.purpose: Execute the SolonCode agent in the current working directory with configured LLM and skills.

mvp_plan: week 1: Implement core CLI command parsing and execution
week 1: Integrate basic LLM communication with config-driven model selection
week 1: Support automatic skill indexing from workspace
acceptance_tests: Agent successfully loads skills from the current workspace and communicates with the configured LLM endpoint.
Configuration file is auto-created under ~/.soloncode/config.yml if missing, and model changes take effect immediately.
Sub-agent mechanism is invoked for delegated tasks without requiring explicit initialization.
User can install SolonCode via one-line script on macOS/Linux and run 'soloncode' from any directory.
